free_text: Light charged particle production in the interaction
of fast neutrons (25-65 MeV) with uranium nuclei
legeres induite par neutrons rapides (En = 25-65 MeV)
sur noyaux lourds (209Bi et natU). Thesis of Univer.
Catholique de Louvain, Facultee des Sciences, Depart.
de Physique. (Full tables for U-nat, Bi-209)
correction procedure by Monte Carlo simulation program
is used to suppress high count rate background
Proton beam focused on a water-cooled 3 mm
Well-defined peak at 62.7 MeV,full width at half
maximum is 2 MeV containing 50% of neutrons, plus
a flat continuum of low-energy neutrons.
Square metallic uranium 6*6 cm2, 0.178 mm thick.
U-238 content is 99.2745%, U-235 content is 0.7200%,
20 to 70 degrees in steps of 10 deg.
Total error includes ERR-SYS and ERR-T
uncertainty in the statistics in the
uncertainty in number of target nuclei, etc.
the experimental points in the spectra are about 5.5%,
8.5%, 15.% and 25% for respectively p, d, t and alpha
at 62.7 MeV. They are mainly given by the statistics
in the spectra. At lower ejectile energies, the thick
uncertainties. For all the other incident neutron
energies (continuum), these values are between 2 and 3
times higher as a consequence of the lower incident
* EN-RSL -half width of the incident neutron energy bin
A Monte Carlo simulation calculates the solid angles
and thick target corrections, including energy losses
in the target and in the dE detector, threshold of the
neutron beam energy width, neutron beam profile,
